title: Finite Cubic Hermite Interpolation Checks

solver_reuse.target: finite cubic Hermite interpolation bad value QF_LRA/Farkas regression seed
solver_reuse.pressure: Exact rational cubic Hermite replay for p(x)=1+x+x^2 on [0,1] at x=1/2 computes hermite_value = 7/4, while a malformed row claims hermite_value = 2, isolating a compact scalar equality conflict for the Farkas route.
solver_reuse.next_step: Promote additional Hermite rows only when they add distinct interval scaling, spline, monotonicity, shape-preservation, floating-point, or theorem-reconstruction pressure; keep Hermite interpolation theory and spline convergence claims in future Lean or numerical-honesty lanes.

graduation_criteria: Encode the fixed polynomial, interval, endpoint values, endpoint slopes, normalized parameter, Hermite basis values, scaled derivative terms, Hermite value, and polynomial value as deterministic rational replay obligations.
Route the false Hermite value through checked QF_LRA/Farkas evidence after replay computes the exact value.
Expose the pack through derivative, polynomial, calculus, real-analysis, numerical-analysis, and learner query surfaces.

Keep Hermite interpolation uniqueness, divided-difference equivalence, error estimates, spline theory, shape preservation, monotonicity guarantees, floating-point evaluation, and general approximation theory marked as future Lean or numerical-honesty work until formal routes exist.
